candidates.title: Stability-Optimal Control Theory of Dynamic Weight Decay
candidates.summary: Prove that alignment-aware WD (binary masking) has a stability cost (Theorem 1) that exceeds alignment benefit in small-batch BN networks; derive the PMP-optimal WD schedule (Theorem 3/PMP-WD) as proportional feedback control on the gradient-to-weight ratio, independently derived from both stochastic PMP and RG beta function theory; add Proposition 1 (EMA aggregation requirement for alignment noise at batch≤256). Validate empirically across ρ regimes (0.05, 0.5, 5.0), architectures (ResNet-20, VGG-16-BN), and optimizer types (AdamW vs SGD with matched ρ). The null result (constant WD wins at standard ρ) is the central theoretical prediction of Theorem 1.
hypotheses: H1: ρ-regime boundary ρ* ∈ [1,5] separates invariant and sensitive WD regimes
H2: Binary masking stability cost > alignment benefit iff AIS < (Cσ²/n)·ΔCSI/λ̄
H3: PMP-WD outperforms constant at high-ρ and high-ρ regimes where stability cost is negligible
H4: Alignment signal requires EMA smoothing (k≥10) for reliable signal at batch=128
H5: Multi-architecture (VGG) null result at standard ρ
H6: 3.7× SGD/AdamW sensitivity ratio is primarily ρ-driven
candidates.pilot_focus: P0-A: VGG parallelization (6 methods × 3 seeds, immediate); P0-B: rho_high full sweep; P0-D: matched-ρ SGD completion; P1-A: PMP-WD pilot at ρ=5.0 (contingent on Gate 2)
candidates.theory_path: Theorem 1: Sun et al. stability recursion + binary mask variance analysis (3 new lemmas); Theorem 2: Grönwall layer-wise CSI bound; Theorem 3: Riccati equation PMP-WD + RG beta function independent derivation (Remark 3.1); Proposition 1: Contrarian alignment noise analysis

novelty_scores.theorem_1: 9/10 — no prior formal binary masking suboptimality bound
novelty_scores.theorem_2: 8/10 — layer-wise CSI generalization bound, incremental extension of Sun et al.
novelty_scores.theorem_3_pmp_wd: 8/10 — dual derivation (PMP + RG) of optimal WD feedback; differentiates from Defazio's AdamC (feedforward vs state-feedback)
novelty_scores.proposition_1: 7/10 — alignment noise design constraint; no prior characterization of minimum aggregation horizon

risks: PMP-WD ≈ constant even at high ρ (30% probability) — mitigated: Theorems 1+2 remain valid even without PMP-WD advantage
rho_high (ρ=5.0) shows null result (30%) — mitigated: reframe as 'invariance is surprisingly robust to ρ' — strengthens BN mechanism claim
Theorem 1 proof gap in independence assumption A3 (25%) — mitigated: weaken to conditional result
VGG other methods show divergence > 0.5% (10%) — interesting positive finding if seen; investigate architecture mechanism

candidates.title: Spectral-Phase-Transition Weight Decay (SPWD): Rank Velocity Feedback
candidates.summary: A WD scheduler conditioned on the rate-of-change of per-layer stable rank (rank velocity v_t). Unlike all existing dynamic WD methods that use gradient-space signals (δ̂_t, ‖g_t‖), SPWD uses a weight-space structural signal (d[‖W‖_F²/‖W‖₂²]/dt). This is the first method to exploit the phase-transition character of WD-driven rank minimization. Addresses the Contrarian's noise concern: stable rank norms are computed as scalars (less susceptible to high-dimensional cosine similarity noise than δ̂_t). If primary candidate's PMP-WD shows no advantage, pivot to SPWD as the novel algorithm. If both work, present SPWD as a complementary structural-feedback approach in a Section 5.8 ablation.
hypotheses: H1-SPWD: SPWD outperforms constant WD on SGD CIFAR-10 ResNet-20 (Phi-non-invariant regime)
H2-SPWD: SPWD produces faster rank collapse to lower effective rank than constant WD
H3-SPWD: WD peaks in SPWD correlate with local maxima of |v_t| (rank velocity peaks)
candidates.pilot_focus: P1-B: SGD CIFAR-10 ResNet-20 × 3 seeds × 200 epochs (18 GPU-hours); compare vs constant, CWD, SWD
candidates.theory_path: Truong & Truong (2026) logarithmic-delay norm hierarchy transitions; Galanti et al. (2022) WD as rank regularizer; Yunis et al. (2024) spectral dynamics; phase-transition tracking via susceptibility analog (|drank/dt|)
candidates.novelty_score: 8/10 — AlphaDecay (NeurIPS 2025) uses static per-module spectral density; SPWD uses dynamic rank velocity. No prior work uses rank velocity as WD feedback.
risks: Rank velocity too noisy at epoch level (35%) — mitigated: EMA with β=0.9 + power iteration for stable rank
SPWD shows no benefit over constant WD even on SGD (30%) — falsifiable negative result that still contributes to the paper
Compute budget insufficient for P1-B (15%) — mitigated: 18 GPU-hours fits in current available compute
candidates.paper_score_range: If primary fails: same as front-runner with SPWD replacing PMP-WD (7.0-8.0); if both work: additional 0.3-0.5 score boost

dropped_candidates.title: Pure Empirical ρ-Regime Characterization
dropped_candidates.reason: Absorbed into the front-runner as the experimental component. The ρ-regime map is now Section 5.4 of the front-runner paper, not a separate candidate. Without Theorem 1-3, a pure empirical paper is 'just a list of null results' per the Contrarian.

dropped_candidates.title: QA-WD (Quadratic Alignment Weight Decay) from RG Beta Function
dropped_candidates.reason: Absorbed into Theorem 3 as an independent derivation (Remark 3.1 / Appendix). λ* = β₀·δ̂²_t is the RG beta function fixed point; it converges with PMP-WD in the moderate-alignment regime. Including QA-WD as a separate algorithm would dilute the paper's algorithmic focus. Retained as theoretical validation in Appendix.

dropped_candidates.title: PID-Weight Decay (Proportional-Integral Alignment Controller)
dropped_candidates.reason: Absorbed into Discussion as a 'directions' mention. The P+I formulation (λ_t = λ_P·(1−δ_t) + λ_I·μ_t) adds a third hyperparameter pair relative to PMP-WD and requires separate convergence analysis. Not pursued given compute/writing constraints.

dropped_candidates.title: Germinal Center Weight Decay Algorithm
dropped_candidates.reason: Absorbed as a biologically-motivated special case of PMP-WD/QA-WD in Discussion section. Not a separate contribution.

dropped_candidates.title: MDL/RLCT-Grounded Effective-Dimension WD
dropped_candidates.reason: RLCT requires expensive second-order computation; not well-defined during training. Future work direction only.

dropped_candidates.title: Phase-Transition-Aware WD via Grokking Pressure
dropped_candidates.reason: Practical signals are computationally prohibitive; no tractable algorithm.
